briefing: Highlighting the growing threat, SAP npm packages face supply chain attacks targeting developers.
Anticipate Anthropic's potential $900B+ valuation round expected within two weeks.
Addressing cybersecurity concerns, the FBI warns about China's uncontrolled hacker-for-hire ecosystem.
IBM announces the launch of AI and Quantum Hubs in Illinois and Massachusetts to drive innovation.
Meta raises its 2026 capex forecast to $145 billion, signaling confidence in the AI sector.

articles.title: The never-ending supply chain attacks worm into SAP npm packages, other dev tools
articles.snippet: Mini Shai-Hulud caught spreading credential-stealing malware The wave of supply chain attacks aimed at security and developer tools has washed up more victims, namely SAP and Intercom npm packages, plus the lightning PyPI package.…
articles.ai_summary: Recent supply chain attacks have targeted SAP and Intercom npm packages, spreading credential-stealing malware. This highlights ongoing vulnerabilities in developer tools.
articles.why_it_matters: Exposes critical security risks in widely used software tools.

articles.title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
articles.snippet: Anthropic is asking investors to submit allocations for the AI company’s latest fundraise within the next 48 hours, according to sources familiar with the matter.
articles.ai_summary: Anthropic is reportedly seeking a valuation of over $900 billion in a new funding round, with investor allocations due soon. This could significantly impact the AI investment landscape.
articles.why_it_matters: Signals strong investor confidence in AI startups amid rising valuations.

articles.title: Good Luck Getting a Mac Mini for the Next ‘Several Months’
articles.snippet: Apple CEO Tim Cook told analysts that AI adoption has happened faster than expected.
articles.ai_summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced that AI adoption has exceeded expectations, leading to supply constraints for the Mac Mini. This reflects the growing demand for AI-capable devices.
articles.why_it_matters: Indicates a shift in consumer demand towards AI-integrated technology.

articles.title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
articles.snippet: Apple said it will be supply-constrained on Mac mini, Studio, and Neo in the next quarter, too.
articles.ai_summary: Apple is facing supply constraints for its Mac Mini, Studio, and Neo due to unexpected AI-driven demand. This may affect availability in the upcoming quarter.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the impact of AI trends on hardware supply chains.

articles.title: Govern your bots carefully or chaos could ensue
articles.snippet: Stop the sprawl! With the average Global Fortune 500 enterprise expected to run more than 150,000 AI agents by 2028, up from fewer than 15 today, there’s plenty of room for chaos. Analyst firm Gartner says that, without proper governance, those agents will multiply and run amok.…
articles.ai_summary: Gartner warns that without proper governance, the number of AI agents in enterprises could skyrocket to over 150,000 by 2028, leading to potential chaos.
articles.why_it_matters: Emphasizes the need for governance as AI adoption accelerates.

articles.title: Firefox maker torches Google for building Prompt API into browser
articles.snippet: Mozilla fears wiring an AI API into Chrome will make the web less open Mozilla has reiterated its opposition to Google's decision to build AI plumbing into its Chrome browser, though rather belatedly now that the technology, known as the Prompt API, is already being tested in Chrome and Microsoft Edge.…
articles.ai_summary: Mozilla criticized Google for integrating an AI API into Chrome, arguing it threatens web openness. This response comes as the technology is already being tested.
articles.why_it_matters: Raises concerns about the implications of AI integration in mainstream browsers.

articles.title: Bot her emails: most modern phishing campaigns are AI-enabled
articles.snippet: KnowBe4 says 86% of phishing it tracked used AI, and inboxes are only the start Give a man a phishing kit and he might get lucky a couple of times; teach an AI to phish and it'll change the landscape, if KnowBe4's latest phishing trends report is accurate.…
articles.ai_summary: A report from KnowBe4 reveals that 86% of phishing campaigns now utilize AI, indicating a significant evolution in cyber threats. This trend could reshape security measures.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the increasing sophistication of phishing attacks through AI.

articles.title: The most severe Linux threat to surface in years catches the world flat-footed

articles.title: Elon Musk confirms xAI used OpenAI’s models to train Grok
